The green is still at cols 21-23. It couldn't move further RIGHT. The green is blocked.
Let me reconsider the entire game. I've been trying to fill both frames, but maybe the goal is something else.

- Middle has a green (the original middle green, now with charcoal center)
- The green with W is the original left green that I moved out of the left frame
Wait, that doesn't make sense. Let me trace the actions more carefully.
Initial state:
- Left green with W at cols 18-20 (inside the left room)
- Middle green with B at cols 27-29 (in the corridor)
- Left frame empty (white interior)
- Right frame empty (white interior)
After moving left green LEFT into the left frame:
- Left frame green with W at cols 12-14
- Middle green with B at cols 27-29
After clicking on the middle green:
- Left frame green with charcoal center at cols 12-14
- Middle green with W at cols 27-29
After clicking on the left frame's green:
- Left frame green with W at cols 12-14
- Middle green with charcoal center at cols 27-29
After moving the left frame's green RIGHT 4 times:
- Left frame green with W at cols 21-23 (moved out of the left frame)
- Middle green with charcoal center at cols 27-29
Wait, but the left frame is now empty (white interior)? Let me check the ascii. Looking at rows 33-35, cols 11-15:
Row 33: [(11, 'c'), (15, 'c')] - left and right edges of the frame
Row 34: [(11, 'c'), (15, 'c')] - left and right edges of the frame
Row 35: [(11, 'c'), (15, 'c')] - left and right edges of the frame
So the left frame is now empty! The green moved out of it.
But earlier, when the green was in the left frame, clicking on it transferred W to it and the middle green got charcoal center. But now the green is out of the left frame, and the left frame is empty.
Hmm, so the game mechanic is:
1. Move the green with W into a frame
2. Click on another green to transfer W to it (the previous green gets charcoal center, marking it as "placed" in the frame)
3. But if you move the green out of the frame, the frame becomes empty again
So the goal is to have both frames filled with greens that have charcoal centers.
But I can't get a green into the right frame because the purple wall blocks horizontal movement.
Wait, maybe I need to click on the right frame to "spawn" a green there? Or maybe I need to click on the purple wall to remove it?
Actually, let me reconsider. Maybe the green CAN move vertically, but only when it's in a certain position. Let me try moving the green UP from its current position (rows 33-35, cols 21-23).
Actually, I already tried UP earlier and it didn't work. But maybe it works from a different position?
